name: GridService may write controls (WMaxLimPct accepted and echoed)
hypothesis: The grant half of the RBAC contract: GridServiceSunSpec holds rw on the commanded control group (704-712), so a WMaxLimPct write must be ACCEPTED and echoed back through the gateway's projection. A grid-service role DENIED a legitimate control write is as much a failure as a read-only role being granted one.
expected: The write is accepted and the readback converges to the commanded value within the SLA.
fix: If a GridService control write is denied, look at configs/rbac/rules.json (its commanded rw grant on models 704-712) and internal/authz/compile.go (the compiled grant set for the role+unit).
notes: Nominal value (100% = no curtailment) so the grant proof never leaves a DER limited. Complements the denial specs and the Go matrix sweep. sla_s=15 (not 5): the 704 WMaxLimPct echo is the reconciler's ControlEcho, which refreshes on the southbound reconcile POLL cadence (~5s). In the full suite this scenario runs immediately after comm-loss-sentinel-mask leaves the secure DER (unit 2) fresh out of comm-loss, so the first post-recovery reconcile cycle that applies+echoes the commanded 100 can take up to ~one full cadence — the write IS accepted and applied, the echo just arrives on the next poll. 5s was under one cadence and flaked (observed: converged in 5.0s, DEGRADED at the boundary; occasionally >5s, FAIL); 15s gives ~3 cadences of margin without masking a real regression (a genuinely denied/unapplied write never converges at all).
